  "findings": [
    "Heat pump O/B reversing valve configured incorrectly",
    "System heats when cooling is called, indicating reversed valve logic"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Verify thermostat model and compatibility with heat pump",
    "Check O/B terminal wiring at thermostat and air handler/furnace",
    "Reprogram thermostat for correct reversing valve type (O or B)",
    "Test system in heating and cooling modes after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "The system is in-service but operates contrary to the thermostat call (heating when cooling is demanded). This is a functional failure caused by incorrect configuration or wiring of the O/B reversing valve, degrading performance and potentially causing comfort and energy-waste issues. Per manufacturer instructions, the reversing valve must be set to match the heat pump's default (typically energized in cooling for 'O', heating for 'B').",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation manuals for thermostat and heat pump",
    "ANSI/ACCA 5 QI-2015 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification) for proper setup and commissioning"
  ]
}
